title: Senior Badminton Ranking Tournament Returns to Tamil Nadu After Five Years
indian_angle: Indian fans will be keen to follow the return of a major domestic senior ranking tournament to Tamil Nadu, a significant event for national players.
summary: The Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026 is scheduled to be held in Chennai from July 16th to 23rd, marking its return to Tamil Nadu after a five-year gap.

title: BAI Releases Eligibility Criteria for World Junior Championships 2026
indian_angle: This circular is crucial for aspiring Indian junior shuttlers who aim to represent the nation at the prestigious World Junior Championships in Cairo.
summary: Badminton Association of India (BAI) has issued a circular detailing the player eligibility criteria for selection to the BWF World Junior Championships 2026, set to take place in Cairo, Egypt.

title: Delhi World Championships: Why Badminton Needs Gen Z, Gen Alpha Watchers
indian_angle: As India prepares to host the World Championships in Delhi, engaging younger generations is vital for the sport's long-term popularity and growth in the country.
summary: An analysis explores the necessity of attracting Gen Z and Gen Alpha audiences to badminton, especially with the BWF World Championships 2026 being hosted in Delhi, to ensure the sport's future appeal.

title: BAI President Sarma on Monkeys Watching Games: "What Is The Problem?"
indian_angle: The statement from the head of India's badminton association provides an unusual insight into the unique challenges and perspectives surrounding domestic tournaments.
summary: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma made a quirky remark about monkeys attending a game, asking "What is the problem if monkeys come to watch a game?", following an incident at the India Open.

title: Indian Shuttlers Must Learn to Win Without a Coach Around
indian_angle: This opinion piece directly addresses a crucial aspect of player development for Indian shuttlers, advocating for greater independence and tactical thinking on court.
summary: An opinion piece suggests that Indian badminton players need to develop more self-reliance and the ability to strategize and win matches independently, even without constant coaching presence.

title: Badminton's 3x15 Scoring System to Debut in Indian Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: The introduction of a new scoring system will significantly impact strategy and play in all domestic tournaments for young Indian players.
summary: The 3x15 scoring system is being implemented across all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships for U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups, set to debut at Ernakulam.
